source_pr.title: disttask: fix failed step is taken as success
source_pr.body:

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and

-->

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?
- see issue. also fix some flaky test
### Check List

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.


symptoms: - see issue. also fix some flaky test
failure_signature: - see issue. also fix some flaky test
root_cause_explanation: The scheduler previously treated a step as succeeded when there were no pending/running subtasks, even if some subtasks had already failed/canceled. Depending on timing (when states/errors are observed), this could incorrectly advance to the next step and make the test behavior unstable. The fix checks per-state counts and only considers a step succeeded when states are empty or all succeed, otherwise it collects and handles subtask errors.
fix_pattern: Determine step success from per-state counts; handle failed/canceled subtasks before advancing steps

evidence: evidence: - see issue. also fix some flaky test
